checks.a_analysis: The output contains a structural placeholder '{"_omitted": "21 sections omitted for token budget"}' which is not in the source document. However, according to the instructions, section labels or structural tags added by the pipeline are considered acceptable AI additions and not fabrications. The metadata title also appears to be generated. No other fabricated body text was found.
The output does not contain any phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs. All text in the included sections was verified against the source, and no fabricated contact information was identified.

The output is missing a significant portion of the source document. Specifically, pages 4 through 18 and pages 21 through 26 are absent from the JSON. This includes major sections such as 'About NCDIT', 'Financial Information', 'Priorities and Goals', and 'Goal Highlights'. The output explicitly notes the omission of 21 sections.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output JSON against the source PDF and found no instances where text blocks or sections were duplicated. The document text flows uniquely from section to section.

The heading hierarchy is broken between Section 19 and Section 20. Section 19 uses an H1 for the main title 'Closing the Digital Divide' followed by H2s for sub-topics. Section 20, which continues the same topic, starts its sub-headings (e.g., 'Satellite Internet Technologies...') at level H3. This results in a skip from H1 to H3 within the logical flow of that topic on the new page/section, creating an inconsistency with the H2 levels established on the previous page for equivalent content types.
checks.b_evidence_source: The sub-headings on page 20 (e.g., 'Satellite Internet Technologies for Student Connectivity Pilot') are at the same hierarchical level as those on page 19 (e.g., 'GREAT Grant Program').
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 20 uses H3 for 'Satellite Internet Technologies for Student Connectivity Pilot' (reading_order 230), whereas Section 19 used H2 for 'GREAT Grant Program' (reading_order 223).

checks.a_analysis: The tables in Sections 19 and 20 are used to represent sidebars containing statistical highlights. The structure (rows/columns) logically maps the data keys (e.g., '1M+') to their descriptions in a way that preserves the informational relationship.

There is a significant structural omission in Section 2 (Page 2). The 'Inside This Report' page contains a grid of icons with text labels. In the output JSON, the text labels (e.g., 'About NCDIT', 'Enterprise Functions and Divisions') are completely omitted from the reading order and elements list. This results in a loss of the primary navigational content for that page.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2 contains text labels under each thumbnail image: 'From the Secretary', 'About NCDIT', 'Vision, Mission and Goals', etc.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 2 (reading_order 5-19) contains the H1 and several images, but no text elements corresponding to the labels found in the source.

checks.a_analysis: While the source document contains bulleted lists (e.g., on page 5 and page 13), these sections are omitted from the provided JSON snippet. In the visible sections (1, 2, 3, 19, 20, 27, 28), no lists are present in the source that were failed in the output.

The source document contains several URLs (e.g., on pages 24 and 25). However, the sections containing these links (Sections 24 and 25) are omitted from the provided JSON snippet. No fabricated links were found in the visible sections.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output effectively converts the multi-page PDF report into a single-column responsive web layout. The visual organization of sections (From the Secretary, About NCDIT, Goal Highlights, etc.) follows the original document's order. Navigation landmarks are added correctly via the Table of Contents. Information is easy to find in its expected relative position.

The document contains limited tabular data. The financial information presented as pie charts in the source is converted into accessible lists in the HTML. The data blocks on page 19 ('Closing the Digital Divide') are rendered as a clean, understandable HTML table. All tabular content is legible and well-structured.

All meaningful images from the source PDF, including the cover art, executive portraits (Jim Weaver, Glenn Mack), the Governor Roy Cooper photo, and all thematic illustrations and icons, are present and correctly rendered in the HTML output.

The content flows logically without duplication of body paragraphs or data sections. Section headings appearing in both the Table of Contents and the main body is expected behavior for accessible navigation.
